q: Alternating current (AC) can be defined as current that continuously varies in which two properties?

a: AC can be transmitted with zero line loss

a: A high concentration of free electrons
b: A very high resistance to the flow of electric current
c: A positive temperature coefficient of resistance only

q: Which type of resistor is highly preferred in high-power DC circuits because of its robust heat-handling capabilities?
A carbon composition resistor is highly valuable in radio-frequency (RF) circuits primarily because it has:
b: Essentially no internal inductance and capacitance

q: A capacitor stores energy in which type of field?
When a capacitor is connected across an AC supply, how does the phase of the current relate to the phase of the voltage?
c: Current is in phase with voltage

q: The absolute value of the impedance in an AC circuit increases when what happens to the flow of AC current?
An inductor stores energy in which type of field?
When an inductor is placed in an AC circuit, what is the phase relationship between the current and the voltage?
c: Current and voltage are in phase

q: What is the primary reason for placing a ferromagnetic core inside an inductor?
a: To decrease the physical weight of the coil
b: To increase the inductance of the coil

q: What is a major advantage of a toroidal core over a solenoidal core in inductor design?
a: The toroid core is much cheaper to wind
b: The toroid confines and concentrates the magnetic flux within its core
c: The toroid allows for a larger air gap
d: The toroid eliminates all copper losses

b: As a single vertical line or pip
c: As a series of decaying harmonic peaks

q: Which statement is true regarding a balanced three-phase AC power system?
a: It consists of three waves of different frequencies
b: It has three waves of the same magnitude separated by 120 phase degrees
c: It requires only a single wire to transmit power
d: The phase difference between any two waves is 90 degrees

q: If two AC waves have the same frequency and the same amplitude but are in opposite phase (180 degrees out of phase), their composite wave is:
a: Twice the amplitude of the original waves
c: Half the amplitude of the original waves

q: If two AC waves have the exact same frequency and are in phase, their composite wave:
b: Has an amplitude equal to the sum of the two original amplitudes
c: Has a frequency equal to twice the original frequency
d: Has a phase shift of 90 degrees

a: The number of turns in the coil
b: The diameter of the wire used to wind the coil

q: How do inductors combine when connected in series, assuming there is no mutual electromagnetic coupling between them?
c: They cancel each other out completely
d: The total inductance equals the average of the individual inductances

q: What is a major physical advantage of using a pot-core winding for an inductor?
a: It completely eliminates copper winding resistance
b: It provides a very large inductance in a small physical volume
c: It allows the core to operate at high temperatures without expanding
d: It can only be used with high-power AC lines

d: The working voltage of the capacitor increases

q: As the spacing between the plates of a parallel-plate capacitor is made smaller, all other factors remaining equal:
d: The leakage current drops to zero

d: Wave Y is in phase opposition to Wave X
